# CHAOS Bootstrap Report

## Purpose

This file is the mandatory audit trail for the `chaos:init` run.

It records how the CHAOS workspace was generated, which confirmations were provided by the human owner, which assumptions remain unresolved, and which sources were used.

## Run metadata

- Command: `chaos:init`
- Mode used: `<default | --auto | --guided>`
- Generated by: `<tool / agent name>`
- Timestamp: `<timestamp if available>`
- Repository root: `<path if available>`


## Toolchain preflight

`chaos:init` must record the result of required tool checks before or during generation.

| Tool | Required | Status | Detected version | Detection evidence | Action taken | Notes |
|---|---:|---|---|---|---|---|
| Git | yes | pass / fail / warn / skipped / unknown |  | `git --version` output summary | none / installed / deferred / continued / aborted |  |
| Node.js | yes | pass / fail / warn / skipped / unknown |  | `node --version` output summary | none / installed / deferred / continued / aborted | must be `>= 20.19.0` for OpenSpec |
| npm | yes | pass / fail / warn / skipped / unknown |  | `npm --version` output summary | none / installed / deferred / continued / aborted |  |
| OpenSpec CLI | yes | pass / fail / warn / skipped / unknown |  | `openspec --version` output summary | none / installed / deferred / continued / aborted | required for OpenSpec-backed workflow commands |

If any required tool is missing, record the user-selected remediation in `Questions asked and user answers`.

No tool may be installed silently.

## Config generation

`chaos:init` must record how `.chaos/config.yaml` was handled.

| Area | Value | Source | Status | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Config file | `.chaos/config.yaml` | generated / existing | created / updated / preserved / skipped |  |
| Project type | `<value>` | repo evidence / user / default | verified / inferred / defaulted / user-provided |  |
| Primary language | `<value>` | repo evidence / user / default | verified / inferred / defaulted / user-provided |  |
| Spec engine | `<value>` | repo evidence / user / default | verified / inferred / defaulted / user-provided |  |
| ADR path | `<path>` | repo evidence / user / default | verified / inferred / defaulted / user-provided |  |
| Decision log path | `<path>` | repo evidence / user / default | verified / inferred / defaulted / user-provided |  |
| Validation commands | `<commands>` | repo evidence / user / default | verified / inferred / defaulted / user-provided |  |
| Protected file policy | `<summary>` | default / existing config / user | verified / inferred / defaulted / user-provided |  |

Record any config conflicts here:

| Conflict | Existing config | Detected evidence | Resolution | User answer / rationale |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `<conflict>` | `<config value>` | `<detected value>` | preserved / amended / deferred | `<answer>` |

Config sanity check:

- Required sections present: yes / no
- No secrets or credentials detected: yes / no / unknown
- No architecture decisions encoded in config: yes / no / needs review
- Protected-file policy avoids silent `AGENTS.md` / root `README.md` edits: yes / no

## Questions asked and user answers

| Question | User answer | Impact |
|---|---|---|
| `<question>` | `<answer>` | `<how the answer changed generated files>` |

If no questions were asked, state why. Example: `--auto mode used and no mandatory confirmation gate was triggered`.

## Scope decisions

| Area / track | Status | Confirmation source | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| Backend | included / context-only / excluded | user-confirmed / inferred / conservative fallback |  |
| Mobile / MAUI | included / context-only / excluded | user-confirmed / inferred / conservative fallback |  |
| Infrastructure / deployment | included / context-only / excluded | user-confirmed / inferred / conservative fallback |  |
| Data / persistence | included / context-only / excluded | user-confirmed / inferred / conservative fallback |  |

Major available tracks must not be excluded without explicit confirmation.

## ADR status handling

| ADR status found | Treatment in CHAOS workspace | Confirmation source |
|---|---|---|
| Proposed | accepted working posture / proposed working posture / context only | user-confirmed / conservative fallback |
| Accepted | accepted posture | source evidence |
| Draft | draft/context only | source evidence |

If any `Proposed` ADR was treated as accepted working posture, record the explicit user confirmation here.

## Source inventory

| Source | Type | Status | Used for | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `<path>` | ADR / README / spec / repo structure / user input | verified / missing / inferred | context / architecture / decisions / rules / gates / README |  |

Definitions:

- `verified`: path/content was inspected during this run.
- `missing`: expected or referenced source could not be found.
- `inferred`: source was inferred from user input, chat context, filenames, or another document, but not directly inspected.

## Conflicts detected

| Conflict | Sources | Resolution status | Required action |
|---|---|---|---|
| `<conflict>` | `<sources>` | unresolved / user-resolved / deferred | `<action>` |

Do not silently resolve conflicts.

- Main reason:
- Required human review:
- Next recommended command: `chaos:status`
